intro: Victorinus of Pettau (d. c. 304), bishop of Poetovio (modern Ptuj, Slovenia) and martyr, is the earliest known Latin exegete. His Commentary on the Apocalypse — the only surviving complete work — is the oldest Latin commentary on Revelation, combining a chiliastic reading with allegory to explain the seven visions. He also wrote commentaries on other biblical books, most now lost.
